prompt: Create a vertical minimalist watercolor painting on off-white rice paper, inspired by restored ancient East Asian ink-wash aesthetics with cinematic softness and visible paper grain. The composition has exactly two large translucent blue-green abstract rock or lotus-leaf forms: one tall rounded form cropped along the left edge and one larger rounded form cropped at the upper right, with a pale blue wash continuing downward on the right. Place exactly one delicate pale pink flower near the top center, partially tucked behind the right blue form, with five soft translucent petals and a small cluster of dark ink dots at its center. Add exactly one small horizontal blue pond-like brush shape in the middle. Draw exactly three very thin black ink plant stems rising from the bottom center, with sparse tiny leaves and a curved dotted branch arcing left-to-right across the center. At the bottom left, include exactly one small red artist seal and one small black calligraphic signature mark. Use misty teal blue, pale pink, off-white, black ink, muted red, minimalist Chinese watercolor and ink wash on textured rice paper, and quiet, airy, poetic, cinematic. Keep the image uncluttered, asymmetrical, softly blurred at the edges, with no frame, no modern objects, and no readable text except the small abstract seal and signature.
